annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":57990,"title":"[Sql] Docs: Update CHANGELOG, README key concepts, and examples for 1.4.0 release","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` **Service directory:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #57982) by `@ArthurMa1978` ## What Changed The 1.4.0 GA release upgraded the REST API version to `2025-01-01` and updated dependent libraries (Azure.Core 1.52.0, Azure.ResourceManager 1.14.0). The release also deprecated the `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` property (marked `[Obsolete]` and `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]`) in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`, which was not documented in the CHANGELOG. The package also carries AOT compatibility (added in beta.2) and marks obsolete all APIs dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 API version. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG 1.4.0 entry is sparse:** Does not mention the deprecation of `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`, or the persistence of AOT compatibility and the 2014-04-01 API retirement warnings through to the GA release - **README Examples section lacks SQL-specific samples:** The section only links to a generic `(aka.ms/redacted) page; two SQL-specific sample files exist at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/` (`Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md`, `Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md`) but are not referenced in README.md - **README Key concepts section is generic:** Only links to a generic Azure SDK concepts page; it should describe SQL-specific resources managed by this library (SQL servers, databases, elastic pools, managed instances, backup policies, audit policies, encryption protectors, etc.) 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `## 1.4.0 (2026-04-09)` section to add missing details: ```markdown ## 1.4.0 (2026-04-09) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to `2025-01-01`. - `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` is AOT-compatible (carried forward from 1.4.0-beta.2). ### Deprecations - `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Use `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` instead. - All classes and methods dependent on the retiring 2014-04-01 REST API version are marked obsolete. See (learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for details. ### Other Changes - Upgraded dependent Azure.Core to 1.52.0. - Upgraded dependent Azure.ResourceManager to 1.14.0. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` **Key concepts section** — replace the current content with SQL-specific information: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` library lets you manage the full lifecycle of Azure SQL resources: - **SQL Servers** (`SqlServerResource`): Create, update, delete, and configure Azure SQL logical servers. - **SQL Databases** (`SqlDatabaseResource`): Create and manage databases within a server, including geo-replicas and point-in-time restore. - **Elastic Pools** (`ElasticPoolResource`): Group databases that share a set of resources. - **Managed Instances** (`ManagedInstanceResource`): Create and manage Azure SQL Managed Instance environments. - **Managed Databases** (`ManagedDatabaseResource`): Databases hosted within a Managed Instance. - **Encryption Protectors** (`EncryptionProtectorResource`): Control Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) keys. - **Audit & Threat Protection Policies**: Configure server/database blob auditing, extended auditing, and Advanced Threat Protection. - **Backup Policies**: Manage short-term and long-term backup retention policies. - **Failover Groups** (`InstanceFailoverGroupResource`): Configure geo-failover groups for high availability. Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` **Examples section** — replace the current content to reference the existing SQL-specific sample files: ```markdown ## Examples The following samples demonstrate common scenarios using this library: - [Managing Managed Instances](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md) — Create, list, update, and delete Azure SQL Managed Instances. - [Managing Managed Databases](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md) — Create, list, and delete Managed Databases within a Managed Instance. Additional code samples can be found in the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet-backed examples in the README are required for this issue. The existing `Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.cs` and `Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.cs` in `tests/Samples/` already have `#region Snippet:` regions used in the `samples/` markdown files. This issue only adds direct links in `README.md` to those already-existing sample markdown files. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` already contains all six required sections. The changes above improve: - **Key concepts** — from a generic one-liner to SQL-specific resource descriptions - **Examples** — from a single generic link to direct links to the two existing SQL sample files plus the generic link No sections need to be added; only existing content needs to be enriched. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 sqlmanagement` — confirm no README changes are introduced (snippets are already injected into `samples/` files, not into README directly) 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 sqlmanagement` — no public API surface changed; this is a documentation-only update 5. `dotnet format sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24181580160/agentic_workflow) · ● 480.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57990","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T09:07:40Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T09:07:40Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0 GA release: a sparse CHANGELOG missing the `SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` → `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB` deprecation entry, a generic README Key Concepts section, and missing links to existing SQL-specific sample files in the README Examples section.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` 1.4.0
**Affected API:** `ManagedInstanceVcoresCapability.SupportedMemoryLimitsMB` (deprecated in favor of `SupportedMemoryLimitsInMB`)
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG 1.4.0 entry does not document the property deprecation or AOT compatibility carried forward from beta
- README Key Concepts section is generic (one-liner link) rather than SQL-specific
- README Examples section links only to a generic page, ignoring the two existing sample markdown files in `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/samples/`

**Root ask:** Update CHANGELOG.md and README.md to accurately reflect all 1.4.0 changes and link to existing SQL-specific samples.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue triggered by PR #57982. No runtime bugs are involved.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Open `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/CHANGELOG.md` and add the missing deprecation notice and AOT/API version retirement notes to the `## 1.4.0` entry.
2. Update the **Key concepts** section of `README.md` to enumerate SQL-specific resource types (`SqlServerResource`, `SqlDatabaseResource`, `ElasticPoolResource`, `ManagedInstanceResource`, etc.).
3. Update the **Examples** section of `README.md` to add direct links to `samples/Sample1_ManagingManagedInstance.md` and `samples/Sample2_ManagingManagedDatabases.md`.
4. Run `dotnet build sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` and `dotnet test ... --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm no regressions from the doc edits.
5. Run `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 sqlmanagement` to confirm no public API surface changes (documentation only).
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — The package `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` clearly belongs to the management-plane SDK family (`Azure.ResourceManager.*`).
- **Service:** `SQL` — Explicitly stated in the issue body and package path (`sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`).
- **Confidence:** High — Both labels are unambiguously indicated by the package name; CODEOWNERS has an exact `%SQL %Mgmt` entry at line 1146.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %SQL %Mgmt` (line 1146) — matches both predicted labels "SQL" and "Mgmt".
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ericshape
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue left unassigned (ServiceOwners-only path). "needs-team-attention" was not added because the issue is bot-filed (not customer-reported).
- **Scan notes:** Entries `%SQL - VM %Mgmt` (1164), `%SQL - Replication & Failover %Mgmt` (1161), `%SQL - Managed Instance %Mgmt` (1158), `%SQL - Elastic Jobs %Mgmt` (1155), `%SQL - Data Security %Mgmt` (1152), `%SQL - Backup & Restore %Mgmt` (1149) were all encountered first in the bottom-to-top scan but did not match (issue has "SQL", not those sub-labels). First full match was line 1146.
</details>
